commonProblems.h2: BMW 4 Series Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The BMW 4 Series delivers refined performance, but its early N47 diesel and N20 petrol engines are widely documented for premature timing chain wear, while modern B48 and B58 units frequently suffer cooling system degradation and plastic housing fatigue. Every rebuilt BMW 4 Series engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components engineered to permanently resolve these original weak points, backed by a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. The decision groups below break down verified UK repair versus replacement costs so you can choose the most reliable, cost-effective path for your specific variant.

problems.h4: BMW 4 Series Timing Chain Failure - Why N47 & N20 Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.rootCause: The rear-mounted timing chain design on early N47 and N20 engines places extreme tension on thin plastic guides and a weak hydraulic tensioner, causing the chain to stretch, rattle, and eventually skip teeth under cold-start conditions.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine-off chain swap, upgraded tensioner installation, reinforced plastic/metal guides, and timing recalibration
repairOptions.longevity: 80,000+ miles; only viable before valve-to-piston contact occurs

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Timing chain replacement plus valve decarbonising, bent valve straightening, and head skimming after initial chain jump
repairOptions.longevity: Restores compression but leaves bottom-end chain tensioner weakness intact

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete engine swap with a fully rebuilt unit featuring reinforced chain guides, updated tensioner, bench-tested performance and 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent solution; upgraded internals eliminate the original factory weak point

problems.recommendation: If your BMW 4 Series is rattling on cold starts but still runs smoothly, a specialist preventative chain replacement with an updated tensioner kit offers excellent value and prevents catastrophic failure. Once the chain has skipped or you hear heavy mechanical knocking, the bottom end is likely compromised and a full rebuilt engine replacement becomes the only reliable, cost-effective option.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt N47D20C & N20B20B BMW 4 Series engine quotes now

problems.group: BMW 4 Series Oil Filter Housing Leak
problems.h4: BMW 4 Series Oil Filter Housing Leak - Why B48 & N55 Engines Lose Coolant
problems.rootCause: Repeated heat cycling causes the factory plastic thermostat housing and oil filter housing gaskets to become brittle, allowing oil to contaminate the accessory drive belt and coolant to escape into the sump or engine bay.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Drain fluids, replace thermostat housing, oil filter housing, updated silicone gaskets and tensioner pulley
repairOptions.longevity: 40,000-60,000 miles; highly recommended before belt failure occurs

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full housing replacement plus alternator, power steering pump or starter motor swap if contaminated oil destroyed the serpentine belt
repairOptions.longevity: Prevents complete breakdown but leaves ageing plastic components in other areas

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine swap with reconditioned unit featuring reinforced aluminium coolant manifolds, metal housing upgrades, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Eliminates all plastic weak points; ideal for high-mileage BMW 4 Series with multiple ancillary leaks

problems.recommendation: Address plastic housing leaks the moment coolant drops or oil appears on the timing cover. A specialist seal and housing upgrade is highly cost-effective and prevents catastrophic serpentine belt failure. If your BMW 4 Series has surpassed 80,000 miles with multiple ancillary leaks, a rebuilt engine with upgraded aluminium manifolds delivers long-term reliability without recurring plastic fatigue.
problems.cta: Get quotes for BMW 4 Series B48 & N55 rebuilt engines today

problems.h4: BMW 4 Series Turbocharger Failure - Boost Loss & Whining Noises
problems.rootCause: High exhaust gas temperatures and factory plastic charge pipe clamps degrade over time, causing boost leaks, turbo shaft wear, and eventual bearing collapse under sustained high-RPM driving or heavy load.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Swap factory plastic intercooler pipes for reinforced silicone/aluminium, replace boost actuator and diverter valve
repairOptions.longevity: Restores boost pressure; does not repair internal turbo wear if whining is already present

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove, balance and replace turbine shaft assembly, seals and wastegate, plus fresh oil and feed line installation
repairOptions.longevity: 50,000+ miles; viable only if the intercooler is thoroughly cleaned of debris and metal

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete engine swap with a dyno-tested rebuilt unit, upgraded metal charge pipes, refreshed oil gallery and 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent performance restoration; eliminates turbo fatigue and oil starvation risks

problems.recommendation: If your BMW 4 Series is experiencing intermittent boost loss without heavy smoke or metallic whining, upgrading to a reinforced charge pipe and actuator kit resolves most early-stage issues affordably. Once the turbo shaft exhibits axial play or the intercooler is contaminated with oil, a full engine replacement becomes necessary to protect the catalytic converters and maintain factory performance standards.
problems.cta: Compare BMW 4 Series turbo-replacement and engine quotes instantly

problems.group: BMW 4 Series EGR Valve & DPF Failure
problems.h4: BMW 4 Series EGR Valve & DPF Failure - Warning Lights & Limp Mode
problems.rootCause: Exhaust gas recirculation valves accumulate carbon deposits that prevent proper closure, causing incorrect air-fuel ratios that rapidly clog the diesel particulate filter and trigger engine management limp mode.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove valve, ultrasonic carbon clean, replace gaskets, perform forced DPF regeneration and update ECU mapping
repairOptions.longevity: 30,000-40,000 miles; effective for early-stage clogging before DPF crystallisation

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Install new Euro 6 compliant DPF, replace EGR cooler and module, ensure emissions system passes MOT testing
repairOptions.longevity: 60,000+ miles; restores compliance but requires consistent motorway driving to prevent recurrence

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine swap with professionally rebuilt unit featuring updated EGR calibration, cleaned intake runners, MoT-ready emissions setup and 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Ideal for high-mileage fleet BMW 4 Series where chronic short trips have caused internal carbon fouling beyond component repair

problems.recommendation: Early carbon build-up is easily resolved with a specialist EGR clean and DPF regeneration service. If your BMW 4 Series displays persistent limp mode or DPF ash blockage warnings, replacing the EGR cooler and filter assembly is the most reliable compliant fix. For engines suffering from severe internal soot contamination, a rebuilt diesel unit with modernised emissions mapping offers superior long-term drivability.
problems.cta: Check rebuilt BMW 4 Series diesel engine prices for your variant

problems.group: BMW 4 Series M4 Crank Hub Slippage
problems.h4: BMW 4 Series M4 Crank Hub Slippage - Track & High-Output Engine Damage
problems.affectedModels: BMW 4 Series M4, M4 Competition (2014-Present, S55B30A & S58B30A)
problems.rootCause: The factory friction-fit crank hub lacks positive mechanical locking, allowing it to rotate independently under sudden high torque loads, causing catastrophic timing desynchronisation and valve-to-piston contact.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine removal, hub replacement with machined dowel-pin or bolt-on retention kit, precise timing realignment
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent mechanical fix; does not repair existing valve damage if timing already slipped

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Timing hub replacement plus cylinder head removal, valve replacement, bent connecting rod inspection and re-machining
repairOptions.longevity: Restores compression but introduces mixed-new/old components in a high-stress M Performance block

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned M Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete engine swap with a specialist-rebuilt S55/S58 unit featuring forged rod bearing upgrade, crank retention fix, dyno-verified output and 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: The only guaranteed solution for high-performance BMW 4 Series; ensures track-ready reliability and eliminates factory weaknesses

problems.recommendation: The crank hub slippage is a known design limitation on early S55 engines; if your M4 still has the factory friction hub, preventative upgrading is strongly advised before track or spirited road use. If timing has already shifted and compression is lost, attempting a piecemeal valvetrain repair risks mismatched tolerances in a high-revving M block, making a fully rebuilt specialist engine the safest long-term investment.
problems.cta: Compare prices for rebuilt BMW M4 S55 & S58 engines now

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your BMW 4 Series
finalCta.paragraph: From timing chain stretch on early N47 diesels to turbo fatigue on modern B48 petrols and crank hub issues on M Performance variants, BMW 4 Series engine faults are well-documented but rarely require main dealer replacement premiums. EngineMarket connects you directly with vetted UK specialists who diagnose your exact fault, supply upgraded rebuilt engines tailored to your variant, and deliver nationwide supply & fit service with transparent pricing. Every unit undergoes rigorous bench testing, features reinforced internal components to eliminate known weak points, and arrives with a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare BMW 4 Series Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket’s UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific BMW 4 Series and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: BMW 4 Series Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every engine displacement fitted to the BMW 4 Series across all UK generations is listed below, complete with compatible factory codes, production years and known failure patterns for accurate replacement matching.

items.title: BMW 4 Series 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0L turbo diesel has been the commercial backbone of the 4 Series lineup since its F32 generation launch, evolving from the rear-chain N47 to the front-chain B47 family. It powers the high-volume 418d, 420d and 425d trims, offering a balance of torque, efficiency and fleet suitability with EfficiencyDynamics calibration. While the newer B47 resolves earlier timing concerns, both variants require strict oil service intervals to maintain turbo and DPF longevity.
commonFailurePoints: guide degradation on early N47 units
EGR cooler clogging on later B47 variants
items.cta: Get quotes for BMW 4 Series 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: BMW 4 Series 3.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The inline-six 3.0L diesel unit delivers effortless torque and long-distance refinement, serving as the premium choice for the 430d, 435d and 440d models across both F and G generations. Featuring twin-turbo or electrically assisted single-turbo configurations, these engines pair seamlessly with xDrive systems for enhanced traction and towing capability. Modern B57 iterations benefit from stepped combustion chambers and improved emissions hardware, though complex ancillary routing can complicate workshop access during major repairs.
commonFailurePoints: high-pressure fuel pump wear at higher mileage
items.cta: Get quotes for BMW 4 Series 3.0L diesel engines

items.title: BMW 4 Series 1.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: Introduced to broaden the entry-level petrol range, the compact 1.5L three-cylinder turbo powers the 418i across late F-generation and current G-generation models. BMW's Valvetronic and twin-scroll turbo technology deliver responsive low-end torque while maintaining urban-friendly emissions and fuel economy. Its modular design shares architecture with larger four-cylinder units, meaning replacement parts are widely available, though the aluminium block demands precise torque-to-yield bolt procedures during installation.
commonFailurePoints: excessive vibration from degraded engine mounts
items.cta: Compare BMW 4 Series 1.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: BMW 4 Series 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0L turbo petrol serves as the volume core of the 4 Series petrol range, transitioning from the N20 series to the highly regarded closed-deck B48 family. Powering the 420i, 428i and 430i trims, these direct-injection units offer strong mid-range punch and smooth power delivery suitable for both daily commuting and spirited driving. The B48 upgrade introduced a reinforced timing chain on the intake side and integrated cooling management, significantly improving long-term reliability over the earlier N20 generation.
commonFailurePoints: high-pressure fuel pump wear on N20 units
items.cta: Compare BMW 4 Series 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: BMW 4 Series 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The inline-six 3.0L turbo petrol represents the pinnacle of naturally aspirated-era evolution and modern twin-scroll performance in the 4 Series, underpinning the 435i, 440i and M440i models. Combining lightweight aluminium construction with advanced direct injection and precise VANOS variable valve timing, these engines deliver linear power curves and a distinctive acoustic signature. Specialist rebuilds frequently address charge pipe weaknesses and crank hub retention on early N55 iterations, while the newer B58 platform focuses on cooling system upgrades.
commonFailurePoints: Plastic charge pipe fractures under boost
crank hub slippage on early N55 applications
items.cta: Compare BMW 4 Series 3.0L petrol engine prices

groups.title: BMW 4 Series Hybrid / MHEV Engine Sizes

items.title: BMW 4 Series 2.0L Hybrid Engines
items.description: The plug-in hybrid variant integrates a liquid-cooled electric motor with the established 2.0L B48 turbo petrol, creating the 430e lineup for ultra-low urban emissions without sacrificing highway capability. This eDrive architecture features a high-voltage battery pack positioned beneath the rear seats and regenerative braking calibration that modifies engine load mapping during coasting phases. Replacement focus typically centres on the combustion module, as the electric drive unit operates as a sealed sub-assembly requiring separate dealer diagnostics.
commonFailurePoints: carbon buildup from frequent EV-only operation
items.cta: Enquire about BMW 4 Series 2.0L hybrid engine availability

items.title: BMW 4 Series 3.0L Hybrid Engines
items.description: The 48-volt mild-hybrid system pairs with the latest generation 3.0L inline-six to deliver seamless stop-start functionality, torque fill during gear changes and enhanced fuel efficiency across the M440i range. A belt-starter-generator replaces the conventional alternator, drawing power from a compact lithium-ion auxiliary battery that requires minimal servicing. Engine replacement on MHEV models demands precise high-voltage safety protocols and software integration to ensure the starter-generator communicates correctly with the updated engine control unit.
items.cta: Enquire about BMW 4 Series 3.0L hybrid engine availability

xDrive all-wheel drive becomes available across diesel and petrol lineups

N20 timing guide plastic degradation causes early catastrophic failures
Excellent chassis dynamics establish strong enthusiast following

faq.h2: BMW 4 Series Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a BMW 4 Series engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt BMW 4 Series engine typically costs £1,226 to £4,800 supply-only, depending on engine code, variant and condition. Diesel N47/B47 units sit at the lower end, while performance B58 or S55 engines command premium pricing. Supply and fit installation adds £650-£1,150 labour depending on workshop location and xDrive complexity.
keyPoints: N47D20C rebuilt engines start around £1,226 for 420d variants
B47D20A modern diesel replacements range £1,400 to £2,800 supply-only
Performance S55B30A M4 engines cost £5,200 to £8,800 rebuilt
All prices exclude fitting labour and vary by supplier warranty terms
Reconditioned units offer mid-tier pricing between used and remanufactured options
items.cta: Compare live BMW 4 Series engine quotes from UK specialists now

items.question: Which BMW 4 Series engines have timing chain problems?
items.answer: Early BMW 4 Series N47D20C diesel and N20B20B petrol engines (2013-2016) are most affected by timing chain wear, with failures commonly occurring between 60,000-90,000 miles. The later B47 and B48 engines relocated the chain to the front, significantly improving long-term reliability and reducing urgent replacement enquiries across the model range.
keyPoints: N47D20C rear-mounted chain design causes tensioner and guide wear
Cold-start rattling is the primary warning symptom before catastrophic failure
B47D20A front-chain update from 2015 onwards resolves most timing concerns
N20B20B petrol units share similar plastic guide degradation risks
Rebuilt engines include upgraded metal guides and revised tensioner kits
items.cta: Get quotes for upgraded BMW 4 Series timing chain replacement engines

items.question: Can I replace my N47 engine with a B47 in my BMW 4 Series 420d?
items.answer: Yes, a B47D20A engine can replace an N47D20C in most BMW 4 Series 420d models, but requires ECU remapping, wiring harness adaptation and specialist verification. The upgrade resolves timing chain concerns and improves emissions compliance, though fitment complexity means professional installation is strongly recommended.
keyPoints: B47D20A shares mounting points with N47D20C in F32/F33 chassis
ECU software update essential for compatibility with newer engine management
xDrive models need transfer case calibration after engine swap
Upgrade typically costs £200-£400 more than like-for-like N47 replacement
Verified compatibility checks prevent costly fitment errors during installation
items.cta: Check B47 upgrade compatibility for your BMW 4 Series variant now

items.question: What warranty comes with a BMW 4 Series replacement engine?
items.answer: Every BMW 4 Series engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty as standard. Premium remanufactured units often carry 24-month coverage, while used engines typically offer 3-6 months. Always confirm exact terms, claim process and labour coverage with your chosen UK specialist supplier before ordering.
keyPoints: Rebuilt and reconditioned engines include 12-month unlimited mileage warranty minimum
Remanufactured units often feature extended 24-month coverage for peace of mind
Used engine warranties vary by breaker, typically 3-6 months parts-only
Labour coverage depends on supply-only versus supply-and-fit service selection
Warranty claims require proof of professional installation and service history
items.cta: Compare BMW 4 Series engine warranty terms from verified UK suppliers

items.question: Is a used BMW 4 Series engine reliable?
items.answer: A low-mileage used BMW 4 Series engine can be reliable if sourced from a verified breaker with compression test results and full service history documentation. However, unknown maintenance intervals, hidden internal wear and lack of upgraded timing components mean rebuilt units often provide better long-term value and comprehensive warranty protection for most private and fleet owners.
keyPoints: Verify donor vehicle mileage via DVLA records before purchasing any used unit
Request compression test results and oil pressure readings from the supplier
Used engines lack upgraded timing components that address known N47 weaknesses
Budget for potential ancillary replacement like turbos, injectors or cooling systems
Minimum 3-month warranty recommended to cover early failure risks
items.cta: Check rebuilt BMW 4 Series engine options with 12-month warranty coverage

items.question: What is the difference between reconditioned and remanufactured BMW 4 Series engines?
items.answer: Reconditioned BMW 4 Series engines receive targeted repairs to worn components like seals and gaskets, while remanufactured units undergo complete strip-down with new bearings, pistons and machining to factory tolerances. Remanufacturing costs more but delivers near-zero-hour reliability and longer warranty coverage for high-mileage or performance variants.
keyPoints: Reconditioned units replace visibly worn parts; remanufactured engines rebuild all critical components
Remanufacturing includes cylinder honing, crank polishing and pressure testing as standard
Reconditioned pricing typically £1,450-£3,100; remanufactured £2,800-£4,800 supply-only
Both options include upgraded timing components to resolve known N47 weaknesses
Remanufactured engines often carry 24-month warranties versus 12-month for reconditioned
items.cta: Compare reconditioned versus remanufactured BMW 4 Series engine prices now

items.question: How long does a BMW 4 Series engine last with proper maintenance?
items.answer: With full service history and timely oil changes, BMW 4 Series B47 diesel and B48 petrol engines regularly exceed 150,000 miles before major work. Earlier N47 and N20 units may require timing chain replacement around 80,000 miles, but rebuilt engines with upgraded components can match modern reliability expectations.
keyPoints: B47D20A and B48B20A engines benefit from front-chain design and improved cooling
Annual oil changes using BMW Longlife-04 spec prevent premature turbo wear
DPF regeneration cycles require regular motorway driving to avoid clogging issues
Rebuilt engines with upgraded guides and tensioners reset the reliability clock
High-mileage fleet 420d models often reach 200,000 miles with specialist care
items.cta: Get quotes for long-life rebuilt BMW 4 Series engines with warranty

items.question: Will a replacement engine affect my BMW 4 Series MOT or emissions compliance?
items.answer: A Euro 6-compliant rebuilt BMW 4 Series engine will pass MOT emissions testing when installed correctly with updated ECU mapping. Ensure your supplier provides emissions documentation and that the replacement matches your vehicle's original Euro standard to avoid compliance issues during annual testing or ULEZ zone entry.
keyPoints: Euro 6 B47D20A and B58B30A engines meet current MoT emissions limits
DPF and AdBlue systems must be functional and correctly calibrated post-installation
ECU remapping may be required to clear fault codes after engine swap
Retain all emissions certification paperwork for MoT tester verification if requested
Non-compliant engines risk MoT failure and potential ULEZ charge liabilities
items.cta: Find MoT-compliant BMW 4 Series replacement engines with emissions documentation

items.question: Can I get supply and fit for my BMW 4 Series engine replacement?
items.answer: Yes, EngineMarket's UK specialist network offers supply and fit packages for BMW 4 Series engine replacements nationwide. This turnkey service includes engine delivery, professional installation, fluid flushing and post-fit diagnostics, typically adding £650-£1,150 to the base engine price depending on variant complexity and workshop location.
keyPoints: Supply and fit removes coordination risks between parts supplier and local garage
xDrive and M4 variants require specialist calibration included in fitting package
Most installations complete within 2-3 working days from engine dispatch
Warranty coverage often extends to labour when using network-approved fitting centres
Post-installation road test and fault code clearance included as standard
items.cta: Find BMW 4 Series supply and fit specialists near your location now

items.question: How do I verify the correct engine code for my BMW 4 Series?
items.answer: Check your BMW 4 Series VIN plate under the bonnet or driver's door jamb for the engine code, or enter your registration on the DVLA website for instant variant identification. Matching the exact code like N47D20C or B48B20B ensures compatibility and prevents costly fitment errors during replacement.
keyPoints: VIN lookup reveals exact engine code, chassis generation and production date
Engine codes are stamped on the block near the oil filter housing
B47D20A replaced N47D20C in 420d models from late 2015 onwards
xDrive variants require additional transfer case compatibility verification during ordering
EngineMarket's registration tool instantly matches your variant to available rebuilt units
items.cta: Enter your reg to identify your BMW 4 Series engine code instantly
